scope: AgenC daemon request and tool cancellation mapped from the donor app-server turn interrupt flow. AgenC exposes dot-separated request.cancel and tool.cancel methods instead of the donor turn/interrupt method, but keeps the same control-plane intent: a client can interrupt in-flight work and pending tool approvals resolve as aborts.
_sourceRootBrandingAllow: branding-scan: allow donor source root path

requiredBehaviors: request.cancel is part of the daemon method registry, TypeScript request union, result map, and published JSON Schema
request.cancel requires a JSON-RPC request id and returns whether an in-flight request on the same connection was cancelled
unknown or already-completed request ids return cancelled=false instead of aborting unrelated work
edgeCases: request ids preserve JSON-RPC string and number identity
request.cancel itself is not registered as the in-flight target it cancels

requiredBehaviors: the dispatcher tracks in-flight requests per JSON-RPC connection only for methods that receive and honor request AbortSignal
request.cancel aborts the tracked AbortSignal and the original request returns REQUEST_CANCELLED
request.cancel returns cancelled=false for non-cancellable daemon methods instead of racing a false cancellation response while server work continues
closing a connection aborts all in-flight requests before command-exec connection cleanup
edgeCases: duplicate in-flight request ids fail closed
cancellation does not cross connection boundaries

requiredBehaviors: tool.cancel is part of the daemon method registry, TypeScript request union, result map, and published JSON Schema
tool.cancel validates the same sessionId and requestId pair as tool.deny
tool.cancel resolves the pending background-agent tool decision with the canonical abort ReviewDecision
tool.cancel routes through the background runner cancellation hook when an active tool is already running
the delegate background runner implements that hook by interrupting the live agent control plane so the run-turn/tool abort signal fires
edgeCases: non-pending request ids fail with the same INVALID_ARGUMENT shape as approve and deny
the public result distinguishes cancellation from denial with decision=cancelled
active agents are interrupted only when the requested tool call id is pending or known in-flight

requiredBehaviors: request-scoped AbortSignal is forwarded into fuzzy file search so an in-flight file walk can stop cooperatively
request-scoped AbortSignal is forwarded into commandExec.start so daemon-owned command processes are terminated on cancellation
existing fuzzy cancellation tokens remain scoped by daemon connection
edgeCases: older fuzzy searches still cancel when a later request reuses the same cancellation token
streaming command exec sessions still require notification-capable connections
